channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Angus Taylor has taken both an economic and a cultural gamble
item.description: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Crabb

media:group.media:description: Last night, Angus Taylor chose change.

item.title: Spirit of Tasmania operator to get $506m government bailout amid bungled ferry rollout
item.description: The announcement comes as TT-Line continues to wrestle with the financial fallout from its bungled delivery of the new Spirit of Tasmania vessels.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: TT-Line says the new surcharge won't be applied to existing bookings.

item.title: Bull sculpture made behind bars has inmates cracking the art world
item.description: Inmates at a maximum-security prison in western NSW have built a steel bull sculpture out of 450 parts to help support their families.
item.dc:creator: Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Supplies are donated from various sources and bought through the proceeds of the arts sale.

item.title: Breaking: Matthew Wale becomes Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot win
item.description: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.
item.dc:creator: Chrisnrita Aumanu-Leong and Nick Sas

media:group.media:description: Matthew Wale speaking outside Solomon Islands Parliament today after being voted in as the country's new prime minister.

item.title: Live: Questions remain on Taiwan and Iran on day two of Trump's visit to China
item.description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are set for another day of ceremony and diplomacy after a major meeting between the pair yesterday. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Martin, Nelli Saarinen, and Iris Zhao

media:group.media:description: China's President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on May 14, 2026.

item.title: National commissioner says review into Kumanjayi Little Baby won't 'cut it'
item.description: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.
item.dc:creator: Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Sue-Anne Hunter has criticised the NT government's choice of leaders for a review into the NT's child protection system.

item.title: Retiring Paralympian reflects on journey from bomb shelter to the Hilton
item.description: Christie Dawes's career spanned more than three decades, representing Australia at seven Paralympics and "countless" marathons.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Murphy

media:group.media:description: Christie Dawes crosses the line at the 2020 Tokyo Paralympic Games in Japan.

item.title: International rules is back, with a twist
item.description: Australia and Ireland will meet in an international women's match under Australian rules to kick off the 2026 AFLW season.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Bláithín Bogue (left) and Jennifer Dunne were both named in the All Australian squad in 2025.

item.title: Deaths of Pacific islander farm workers ripple through small country town
item.description: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Loney

media:group.media:description: Two crosses have been placed at the scene where two farm workers died near Manjimup on May 1.

item.title: Driver dies after car rolls over onto railway line
item.description: A man has died after a serious crash on the Sunshine Coast this morning.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dye, Madeline Grace, and Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: A man has been killed after a serious crash at Kulangoor on Queensland's Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Vaccine program slashes RSV hospitalisations of young babies
item.description: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Gribbin and Rachel Carbonell

media:group.media:description: Katryna Cygler (left) had the RSV vaccine while pregnant.

item.title: Teenagers arrested during alleged arson attack on Melbourne CBD bar
item.description: Police allege Bar Bambi in AC/DC Lane has again been targeted by arsonists, while officers also investigate suspicious truck fires at two businesses in Melbourne's inner-west.

media:group.media:description: Police arrested two teenagers who allegedly smashed a window at Bar Bambi and poured petrol inside.

item.title: Firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc
item.description: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.
item.dc:creator: Eden Hynninen and Jean Bell

media:group.media:description: Fire authorities were conducting some planned burns during the autumn months in parts of the Otways, including near Gellibrand.

item.title: Protesters say they are in chains at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment
item.description: A protester has told 891 ABC Adelaide she has chained herself to a gate on War Memorial Drive and that four others have chained themselves to machinery inside the North Adelaide Golf Course.

media:group.media:description: The ABC has been told as well as protesters standing outside the golf club, more have entered the site.

item.title: Why millennials and gen Z feel ripped off by this budget
item.description: The prime minister said this budget was about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Commentators online say the sweeping tax changes in the federal budget advantage Boomers.

item.title: Melanie Lynskey joins the fight for justice after NZ's Pike River Mine disaster
item.description: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.
item.dc:creator: Stephen A Russell

media:group.media:description: Twenty-nine men died in 2010 after a series of explosions in the Pike River Mine. The youngest had just turned 17.

item.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA
item.description: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Talbot Bay tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jackson

media:group.media:description: Big wave surfer Anthony Walsh was the first to ride the tidal rapid.

item.title: WA Premier Roger Cook treads fine line in 'standing up to Canberra'
item.description: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: Roger Cook says his government will stand up to Canberra when it needs to.

item.title: Man charged with murder over mother's death at inner north Canberra home
item.description: The man has been held in police custody since being arrested at the scene and is due in court today.

media:group.media:description: A large area around the home on Dryandra Street was cordoned off yesterday as forensic officers were on the scene.

item.title: Live: US vote to halt Iran war narrowly defeated
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Georgie Hewson

media:group.media:description: Emergency responders gather around a burning vehicle at the scene of an Israeli airstrike in the coastal town of Jiyeh, south of Beirut, Lebanon, Wednesday, May 13.

item.title: Min Woo Lee on top of closest ever PGA Championship leaderboard
item.description: Australian Min Woo Lee enjoys a share of the first-round clubhouse lead, but Masters champ Rory McIlroy has endured a rough start to the PGA Championship.

media:group.media:description: Min Woo Lee has a share of the clubhouse lead.

item.title: Playful seals looking for a feed increasingly snared by fishing lines
item.description: Data from the wildlife rescue group ORRCA shows an increase in the number of fur seal entanglements as they seek out fishing lines in attempts to find an easy meal.
item.dc:creator: Toby Hemmings and Alasdair McDonald

media:group.media:description: The inquisitive nature of pinnipeds is part of why they are more vulnerable to macroplastic pollution, according to a new study.

item.title: Live: ASX opens higher as Wall Street rallies with records
item.description: The ASX opens higher in morning trading after rallies on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in December 2022.

item.title: Ten still in hospital after bus rollover in north Queensland
item.description: Queensland police have described a "confronting scene" at a bus crash on a remote stretch of the Bruce Highway on Thursday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: The bus rolled off the roadway on the Bruce Highway.

item.title: Indigenous housing collapse puts families at risk of homelessness
item.description: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.
item.dc:creator: Tim Little and Zara Margolis

media:group.media:description: Kerry Major faces possible eviction after the corporation she rents from racked up unpaid rates.
